rulings.Case Title: TRICO PIPES ET AL VS. WINNIE WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS TRICO PIPES AND ARAM HODESS'S Motion To Amend Complaint. Plaintiffs TRICO Pipes and Aram Hodess's motion to amend complaint is granted. Plaintiffs have dismissed judgment debtor Wayne Wong as a party defendant, and propose to amend their claims against defendant Winnie Wong. Their proposed claims against Ms. Wong do not appear to be barred by Mr. Wong's discharge or by the bankruptcy stay. In any event, Defendants may raise any challenge to the validity of the amended complaint by way of demurrer or motion for summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: POODLES, INC., VS. ROGER C. KUHN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' All Animals Properties, Lp, 1333 Ninth Avenue Llc, And All Animals Emergency Hospital, Inc'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 1, Defendants All Animals Properties, LP, 1333 Ninth Avenue LLC, And All Animals Emergency Hospital, Inc.'s Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Greg Groeneveld, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The parties are notified that Greg Groeneveld and Mr. Perdue were opposing counsel in Chen v, Tran, CGC-06-455059. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to greg@groeneveldlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MIRA BLANCHARD VS. FLUENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS MIRA BLANCHARD, RYAN COOPER, AND MARK DAVIS' MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER. Plaintiffs' motion to strike Defendants Fluent LLC, Reward Zone USA LLC, RewardsFlow LLC, American Prize Center LLC, and Mohit Singla's "Notice of Filing Answer" is granted. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.90(a)(2) and California Rule of Court 2.111, Defendant must file an answer with the name of this court and the case number on it within 30 days of remand from federal court. A "Notice of Filing Answer" is not the same thing as an answer. Defendants' reliance upon Laguna Village, Inc. v. Laborers' Int'l Union of North America (1983) 35 Cal.App.3d 174, 182 is misplaced because that case merely prevents Plaintiffs from taking Defendants' default; it does not stand for the proposition that the "Notice of Filing Answer" in federal court is a proper replacement for an answer in state court. Defendants shall file an answer with this Court in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 430.90(a)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO ORDONEZ ET AL VS. JOSE A. APLICANO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Scott D. Bleifer In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT JOSE APLICANO's Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: BETTY DUNCAN VS. FLUENT LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS BETTY DUNCAN, ROBERT CHATFIELD, AND DAVID BOMBERGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs' motion to strike the prayer for attorneys' fees in Defendants Fluent LLC, Reward Zone USA LLC, RewardsFlow LLC, American Prize Center LLC, and Mohit Singla's answer is granted. California follows the American rule, which provides that each party to a lawsuit must ordinarily pay his own attorney fees. (Trope v. Katz (1995) 11 Cal.4th 274, 278.) Attorney fees are recoverable by a prevailing party only when authorized by contract, statute, or law. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1033.5(a)(10).) Defendants have not pled any contractual or statutory basis for attorneys' fees, and admit they have not determined whether "a basis for recovery of attorney fees exists." Accordingly, Plaintiffs' motion to strike lies. (See California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Guide 2018) 7:183.) Should Defendants discover a statutory or contractual basis for attorneys' fees, they may seek leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY DUNCAN VS. FLUENT LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF BETTY DUNCAN, ROBERT CHATFIELD, DAVID BOMBERGER DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs' demurrer to Defendants Fluent LLC, Reward Zone USA LLC, RewardsFlow LLC, American Prize Center LLC, and Mohit Singla's answer is overruled as to all thirty-nine affirmative defenses because they may turn on factual issues that cannot be decided on a demurrer. The primary function of the answer is to give the other party notice so that it may prepare its case. (Harris v. City of Santa Monica (2013) 56 Cal.4th 203, 240.) A purported defect in a pleading that otherwise gives notice to the other side cannot be said to affect substantial rights or cause prejudice. (Id.) If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation, Plaintiffs contend that Defendants lack factual support for some or all of the affirmative defenses, Plaintiffs should request that Defendants withdraw those affirmative defenses. If Plaintiffs are dissatisfied by the response, then they may bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking factual support. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANGES PAK VS. GITHUB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Allow Plaintiff To File A First Amended Complaint For Violation Of Equal Pay Act And Retaliation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, April 24, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF ANGES PAK'S Motion For Order To Allow Plaintiff To File A First Amended Complaint For Violation Of Equal Pay Act And Retaliation. Plaintiff Agnes Pak's motion for order to allow Plaintiff to file a first amended complaint for violation of Equal Pay Act and retaliation is granted. The liberal policy of amendment supports the motion. Plaintiff need not prove the corrected allegations she seeks to make, but need only specify those matters listed in Rule 3.1324, including when the facts giving rise to the amendment were discovered. Defendant Github, Inc. has not shown that the minor changes Plaintiff seeks to make in her complaint fall within the sham pleading doctrine. "Critically, the sham pleading doctrine 'cannot be mechanically applied.'" (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (Mar. 28, 2019) 2019 WL 1396740 at *8.) In particular, the doctrine "was not intended to preclude plaintiffs from providing additional and noncontradictory allegations." (Leasequip, Inc. v. Dapeer (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 394, 405 n.6; see also Zakk v. Diesel (Mar. 25, 2019) 2019 WL 1349530 at *11-12 [allegations regarding contract claims in third amended complaint "merely clarifies the basis for [plaintiff's] claims for relief" and sham pleading doctrine therefore did not apply].) Nor does Defendant show that it will be prejudiced if the amendment is allowed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
